title: Machine Learning Learning Roadmap
description: A complete, staged path from beginner to expert — math, statistics and programming foundations, data preprocessing and feature engineering, core ML concepts, supervised and unsupervised learning, ensembles, evaluation, neural networks and deep learning, specialized domains, MLOps and advanced research.

stages.title: Mathematics for Machine Learning
stages.summary: Linear algebra, calculus, probability, statistics and optimization.

nodes.title: Linear Algebra
nodes.description: Vectors, matrices, decompositions and the math of data.

nodes.title: Calculus
nodes.description: Derivatives, gradients and the math behind optimization.

nodes.title: Probability
nodes.description: Random variables, distributions and Bayesian reasoning.

nodes.title: Statistics
nodes.description: Descriptive and inferential statistics for data analysis.

nodes.title: Mathematical Optimization
nodes.description: Objective functions, convexity and numerical optimization.

nodes.title: Information Theory
nodes.description: Entropy and divergence measures used throughout ML.

stages.title: Programming & Tooling for ML
stages.summary: Python, NumPy, Pandas, visualization, data access and dev workflow.

nodes.title: Python for Machine Learning
nodes.description: Core and intermediate Python plus the ML tooling ecosystem.

nodes.title: NumPy
nodes.description: N-dimensional arrays and vectorized numerical computing.

nodes.title: Pandas
nodes.description: Tabular data structures, wrangling and time series.

nodes.title: Scientific Computing
nodes.description: SciPy and symbolic math for numerical work.

nodes.title: Data Visualization
nodes.description: Plotting libraries and visual communication best practices.

nodes.title: Data Access & Storage
nodes.description: Databases, APIs, web scraping and data file formats.

nodes.title: Development Workflow
nodes.description: Version control, command line, code quality and reproducibility.

stages.title: Data Preprocessing & Wrangling
stages.summary: Collecting, cleaning, transforming, balancing and splitting data.

nodes.title: Data Collection & Types
nodes.description: Data sources, data types and assessing data quality.

nodes.title: Data Cleaning
nodes.description: Missing values, outliers, duplicates and error correction.

nodes.title: Data Transformation
nodes.description: Scaling, encoding and mathematical transforms of features.

nodes.title: Handling Imbalanced Data
nodes.description: Resampling, algorithmic and evaluation strategies for class imbalance.

nodes.title: Data Splitting
nodes.description: Train/validation/test splits and avoiding data leakage.

nodes.title: Preprocessing Special Data Types
nodes.description: Text, image, time series and audio preprocessing fundamentals.

stages.title: EDA & Feature Engineering
stages.summary: Exploring data and creating, selecting and reducing features.

nodes.title: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
nodes.description: Understanding data through univariate, bivariate and multivariate analysis.

nodes.title: Feature Engineering
nodes.description: Creating and transforming features to improve model performance.

nodes.title: Feature Selection
nodes.description: Filter, wrapper and embedded methods to select informative features.

nodes.title: Dimensionality Reduction
nodes.description: Linear and nonlinear techniques to reduce feature dimensionality.

nodes.title: Feature Engineering Workflow
nodes.description: Pipelines, automation and reuse of engineered features.

stages.title: Core ML Concepts
stages.summary: Fundamentals, workflow, bias-variance, losses, regularization and optimization.

nodes.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
nodes.description: What ML is, types of learning, problem types and terminology.

nodes.title: The ML Workflow
nodes.description: The end-to-end lifecycle and the model training process.

nodes.title: Bias-Variance Tradeoff
nodes.description: Bias, variance, overfitting, underfitting and generalization.

nodes.title: Loss & Cost Functions
nodes.description: Objectives that quantify and drive model learning.

nodes.title: Regularization
nodes.description: Techniques that control complexity and reduce overfitting.

nodes.title: Optimization & Training
nodes.description: Gradient descent, learning rates, optimizers and convergence.

stages.title: Supervised Learning — Regression
stages.summary: Linear, polynomial, regularized, generalized and nonlinear regression.

nodes.title: Linear Regression
nodes.description: Simple and multiple linear regression, assumptions and diagnostics.

nodes.title: Polynomial & Spline Regression
nodes.description: Modeling nonlinear relationships with polynomials and splines.

nodes.title: Regularized Regression
nodes.description: Ridge, Lasso and Elastic Net for controlling overfitting.

nodes.title: Generalized Linear Models
nodes.description: Link functions, count regression and robust regression.

nodes.title: Nonlinear Regression Algorithms
nodes.description: SVR, tree-based, instance-based and Gaussian process regression.

nodes.title: Regression Model Evaluation
nodes.description: Metrics, residual analysis and validation for regression.

stages.title: Supervised Learning — Classification
stages.summary: Logistic regression, KNN, Naive Bayes, SVM, trees and discriminant analysis.

nodes.title: Logistic Regression
nodes.description: Binary, multiclass and regularized logistic regression.

nodes.title: K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)
nodes.description: Instance-based classification by neighborhood voting.

nodes.title: Naive Bayes
nodes.description: Probabilistic classifiers based on Bayes theorem.

nodes.title: Support Vector Machines (SVM)
nodes.description: Margin-based classifiers and the kernel trick.

nodes.title: Decision Trees
nodes.description: Tree-based classifiers, splitting criteria and pruning.

nodes.title: Discriminant Analysis
nodes.description: Linear and quadratic discriminant analysis classifiers.

nodes.title: Multiclass & Multilabel Classification
nodes.description: Strategies for multiple classes and multiple labels.
